text:
Market today: Mild risk-on mood. Falling oil (Iran talks progress) eases inflation fears, and Kalshi traders see inflation peaking - both tailwinds for rate-sensitive tech/chip names. No macro shock today, but USMCA uncertainty adds background noise for anything with supply-chain exposure.

Your holdings: SOXL (-13.27%) is your biggest bleed - it's a 3x leveraged decaying ETF and margin makes that hurt double; stop at $200, watch it closely. SPAL (-8.15%) is brand-new, very volatile - still finding its legs and decaying by design; stop $21.38 is your line. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec down hard - smallest name, highest risk, least liquidity.

Watch: (1) Whether chip names (SMH, SOXL) catch a bid if inflation expectations keep cooling - that's the real lever for your book. (2) SOXL's $200 stop - a close below that on margin is a real problem.

Bottom line: Your leveraged losers (SOXL, SPAL, PTNM) are the only fire to manage today - everything else is holding fine.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - honor it, no exceptions on margin. If it's approaching that level intraday, consider trimming now rather than waiting for a hard stop-out.
PTNM: Down 15%+ with thin liquidity and no catalyst visible today. Consider whether the position size justifies the risk on a margin account - trim or exit if conviction is low.
No new buys today - macro backdrop is okay but nothing in the scanner has a fresh catalyst strong enough to justify adding leveraged exposure when you already have bleeding positions.
